domain: Sifaka lemur lateral-hop bipedal locomotion (cannot run forward due to long hindlimbs; instead hops SIDEWAYS bipedally for efficient terrestrial travel; energy-conserving because anatomy mismatches forward gait)
specific_mechanism: When default direction (forward gait) is anatomically poor, switch to ORTHOGONAL direction (sideways hop); elastic recoil transfers stride energy laterally.
llm_application: SIFAKA-HOP: an orthogonal-direction optimizer trigger — at each step, if estimated gradient direction has condition-number ratio (largest/smallest eigenvalue of local Hessian estimate) above threshold τ, REPLACE the descent direction with an ORTHOGONAL-COMPLEMENT vector derived from accumulated momentum (project momentum onto null-space of gradient and step there); otherwise standard step. Effectively trades 'forward progress on noisy gradient' for 'lateral exploration via momentum reuse' when the gradient is poorly-conditioned. Different from preconditioning (which still steps in gradient direction, just rescaled).
novelty_claim: AdaGrad/RMSProp/Adam scale gradients per-coordinate (still in gradient direction). Muon/Shampoo precondition by approximate Hessian inverse (still gradient direction). Spectra spike-aware suppresses spectral components but still steps in (suppressed) gradient direction. SAM steps to flat minima via perturbation. SIFAKA-HOP differs by EXPLICIT ORTHOGONAL-DIRECTION step when conditioning is poor — replacing gradient direction with momentum-projected null-space direction.
